entry.title: Are you looking for learning material related to SAP Green Ledger and Financial Accounting ?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering Sustainability for SAP Finance learning journey.

Overview
Sustainability is essential in today's economy and should be a primary focus of your business operations. Discover how SAP Green Ledger and Financial Accounting can monitor, evaluate, and report on the environmental impact of all your operations. Consultants and business professionals dedicated to advancing sustainability will find this learning opportunity to be valuable.

Learning objectives
This course will provide you with an understanding of how SAP Green Ledger relates to Financial Accounting. It's especially important for those who are new to using SAP Sustainability solutions to grasp the business advantages of SAP Green Ledger and how it aligns with your financial data. You'll also dive into key features, integration aspects, analytic capabilities, and planning.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Unlock Your SAP BTP Potential: Transformative Benefits of Functional and Technical Assistance
entry.content:
Are you struggling with the complexities of the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and seeking personalized guidance? Look no further! Functional and Technical Assistance Service for SAP BTP  is here to help you tackle these challenges by offering help to you to ensure that you are following SAP best practices and guidelines when transforming your solutions to the cloud. You get scenario-based assistance to stay on the right track and mitigate any risks that may come your way. The personalized guidance that you receive during this service will help you to adopt the solution and realize the business benefits sooner.

  1. Answering your Specific Questions
    One of the most significant benefits of this service is the ability to get answers to your specific questions. Whether you're dealing with a technical issue or just seeking advice on how to best utilize the platform, the experts at SAP are here to provide clear, tailored solutions. This personal touch ensures that you're not left in the dark and can move forward with confidence.
  2. Fast access to SAP experts
    With the Functional and Technical Assistance Service, you gain access to a dedicated team of SAP experts who are committed to your success. These experienced professionals provide guidance and support to help you navigate the complexities of SAP BTP and ensure your system is running smoothly. Whether you need assistance with configuration, customization, or troubleshooting, our experts are here to help you every step of the way.
    With this service, you're not stuck waiting around for help. SAP understands that time is money, and their team is dedicated to providing prompt, efficient support. No matter how complex your issues may be, the skilled professionals at SAP are on standby to assist you without delay by mapping your question to our cloud-based framework ensuring fast time-to-value.
  3. Flexible Interaction Model
    the service's flexible interaction model allows you to engage with experts in a way that works best for you. Whether you prefer one-on-one consultations, group workshops, or something in between, SAP's experts are ready to adapt to your preferred style of communication. This flexibility makes it easier than ever to get the help you need, when you need it.
  4. Scaleable Offering
    The offering is designed for scalability, allowing customers to easily purchase and adjust the level of assistance they need as their business requirements change. Availability through SAP Store means that the customers can bypass the traditional engagement process with an SAP account team and avoid lengthy contract negotiations. The service is offered at a fixed scope and price for each relevant country, with all pricing and scope details easily accessible on the SAP Store page. Customers can initiate the purchasing process directly from the page, making it simple and hassle-free to access the support they need.

From a customer perspective, there are several use cases where this new offering can prove to be invaluable.

  1. Application Development: When developing a new unique application using SAP Build, businesses can leverage the expertise of SAP consultants to ensure that the application meets their specific requirements and serves its intended purpose effectively. By understanding the nuances of SAP Build, consultants can guide businesses through the entire development process, from ideation to deployment, ensuring that the end product aligns with the organization's goals and objectives.
  2. Application Integration: in the realm of application integration, businesses can utilize SAP Logon within their applications to seamlessly connect with SAP systems and data. The service can provide insights on best practices for integrating SAP Logon, enabling businesses to optimize their application's performance and improve the overall user experience.
  3. Planning & Analytics: when it comes to planning and analytics, setting up and connecting with SAP Datashere can be a complex task. However, with the assistance of experienced SAP consultants, businesses can ensure that they are following best practices for setup and connectivity, ultimately enabling them to make informed, data-driven decisions and derive valuable insights from their SAP Datashere.
  4. Clean Core: the offering provides several benefits:
    1. It helps to identify and address any performance bottlenecks or inefficiencies in legacy code and gives advice on SAP BTP re-implementation, leading to improved system stability and reliability. This, in turn, allows you to focus on leveraging the full potential of the platform to drive your business forward.
    2. A clean core ensures that your SAP BTP implementation is aligned with best practices and industry standards, which is crucial for long-term success and scalability. By optimizing your core system, you can future-proof your SAP BTP implementation and ensure that it can support your business growth and innovation initiatives.

Overall, SAP's Functional and Technical Assistance Service for SAP BTP is a game-changer for businesses looking to maximize their investment in the platform. With personalized answers, quick access to experts, and flexible interaction, you'll be equipped to tackle any challenge that comes your way. Head over to the SAP Store and take advantage of this incredible offering today!

entry.title: Time bound CPM feedback requests - Customer influence - Please vote.
entry.content:
Hello SF community,

Continuous Feedback (CPM) is a very useful tool available on the SAP platform, used extensively to request and provide constructive and timely feedback to employees. Timely feedback reinforces positive behaviour and also impacts quality assessment. We have had instances where the feedback request has not been responded to for long durations or not at all. This results in an endless wait for the requestor and eventually they tune out of the conversation.

We suggest that the CPM feedback requests should be time bound with the option to configure the time duration. Also,  there should be a configurable reminder in place for a pending feedback request. Once the duration is crossed, the feedback request should get ‘auto declined’. This will result in a cleaner slate for the user(s) TO DO list and would put an end to the requestor’s long and at times a never-ending wait.

There is a customer influence for this, more details can be read here - Customer Influence . If you think this will be helpful for you, please vote for this customer influence idea. 

Kind Regards

HSBC

entry.title: Re: 'Forward as applicant' function to manually created p...
entry.content:
Forwarding as an applicant is available only when the candidate is on a requisition and all mandatory information is filled in the application. You cannot directly forward a candidate as an applicant from the candidate database, as the permission is defined in the candidate application.

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: Reading Vehicle Licence Plate (Vehicle Number Plate) Using Document Information Extraction
entry.content:
I am working on a project where I am using Document Information Extraction technology to extract text from vehicle number plates. This technology helps recognize and pull out the alphanumeric characters (text) displayed on number plates. However, I am facing challenges when it comes to extracting the color of the number plate. While the text extraction is not working properly and the color detection part isn't being detected, and the results are not accurate. I need help to improve this process so that I can reliably capture both the text (numbers and letters) and the color of the number plate from images.

I am looking for advice, suggestions, or best practices that could help resolve this issue and make the system work more efficiently.

Specifically, I want to ensure that both the text and color extraction happen smoothly and accurately in my project.
@tomasz_janasz @MariaGoepfert

entry.title: SAP Business Suite – The Integrated Powerhouse for Seamless Product Lifecycle Management
entry.content:
As businesses across all industries navigate the competitive landscape of today's economy, they are realizing the vital importance of effective product data management. To truly outpace competitors, it's essential to have a solution that seamlessly orchestrates the product definition throughout your organization.

Orchestration requires a new era of enterprise management, and the SAP Business Suite is leading the way with unparalleled applications to link your entire supply chain processes with the rest of your enterprise.  Fueling your enterprise with unmatched data that is integrated real-time with operational and business data ensuring every decision is guided by accurate, and in-the-moment insights. Powered with embedded AI to deliver productivity and efficiency across your enterprise.

Let’s explore further how the SAP Business Suite is a comprehensive system of record for managing and all product data for an organization and how this approach allows for value realization across the supply chain.

Simplifying Product Data Management

SAP provides an ERP-driven product data management solution that leverages the integrated and innovative capabilities of SAP Business Suite. This integrated approach prioritizes collaboration, compliance, and sustainability, and transforms how industries handle product information in process and discrete industries.

The SAP strategy for our solutions is centered around customer choice. Within the product lifecycle management market category, we will have the following focus areas:

  • Product Data Management as part of our Business Suite and
  • Integration with external PLM and CAD systems

Overview Product Lifecycle Strategy with SAP Solutions

This strategy provides the ultimate flexibility for customers to choose how they want to unlock the potential that exists from performing product data management with the SAP Business Suite. We see three areas where SAP is uniquely positioned to simplify and streamline your end-to-end supply chain processes, including support for hybrid scenarios, regardless of your industry:

  • Direct CAD Integration: for discrete industry customers needing direct CAD tool integration to manage design files and who want to create and manage product data and product structures through a seamless connection to the SAP Business Suite for execution.
  • Product Data Management: for those customers in both process and discrete industries, who want a native cloud-based product data management platform to create and manage their product definitions with a seamless connection to the SAP Business Suite.
  • PLM System Integration: Ideal for those customers with existing external PLM systems who want a bi-directional flow of product information into the SAP Business Suite to ensure smooth communication and coordination across different systems.

Let’s now dive a bit deeper into examples of these scenarios.

Collaborative Product Data Management for Discrete Industries

Customers in discrete industries have needs to not only manage and control product data that defines their products, but also to directly integrate their authoring tool systems (MCAD and ECAD).  By providing a flexible approach to directly manage their design files coming from various authoring tools, customers can seamlessly create the product structures needed in the SAP Business Suite.  Doing so facilitates the ability to collaborate on this data and to handover the product definition for planning, sourcing, manufacturing, and maintenance.

Direct CAD Integration and Product Data Management

Our focus for these customers is to continue to enhance essential product data management capabilities within the SAP Business Suite and to extend these capabilities beyond the SAP Business Suite with differentiating capabilities, such as collaboration, product structure management and product handover and others. This natively integrated approach fosters not only the efficient sharing of product data across the value chain, but also supports the bi-directional change management process critical for all organizations.

Recipe Formulation and Specification Management for Process Industries

Customers in process industries have needs to manage and control the data that defines their products, but instead of this data being in the form of parts and bills of materials, they are dealing with formulated products where their product data is defined by specifications, recipes and formulations. Not only do they want to manage and control this information, but they also want to easily collaborate internally and externally on it and then easily handover the product definition into ERP system for execution.

Recipe Formulation and Specification Management for Process Industries

For process industries, we are enhancing our capabilities to support these customers and how the create and manage the recipe formulation and specification management processes. All while maintaining full native integration with SAP Business Suite, where the SAP offering stands out due to its integration into compliance, sustainability, and network functionalities. This ensures meticulous product data management that adheres to industry regulations and sustainability targets and efficient sharing of product data across the value chain, but also supports the bi-directional change management process critical for all organizations.

Integration with Leading Third Party PLM and CAD Solutions

Lastly, there are customers who have taken a decision for an external PLM system like Siemens Teamcenter, Dassault Systèmes 3DEXPERIENCE, PTC Windchill or Autodesk Vault to store product design data that also may be originating from an authoring tool. These customers also want to have their product design information seamlessly flow into the ERP environments to streamline supply chain process.

Integration with Leading 3rd Party PLM and CAD Solutions

SAP supports these customers as well by providing a proven and standards-based integration solution that allows for them to have a single source of truth for product data within the SAP Business Suite, enabling not only the efficient sharing of product data across the value chain, but also the bi-directional change management process for the organizations. Additionally, these customers can leverage additional SAP capabilities to support potential hybrid scenarios such as supplier collaboration and visualization.

Customer Choice with SAP

Leveraging the SAP Business Suite as your product data management backbone ensures that all aspects of product data are efficiently managed and utilized. By offering tailored solutions for both discrete and process industries, SAP empowers businesses to streamline their product data management processes, ensuring a single source of product truth for the enterprise.

SAP Business Suite goes beyond traditional PLM systems by integrating seamlessly product data with essential business tools and processes, driving efficiency and coherence throughout the organization. Our solutions focus on their unique strengths as integrated components rather than standalone systems, ensuring a comprehensive approach to product data management. While our support for hybrid scenarios further enhances flexibility and collaboration across the value chain.

Embark on a transformative journey with SAP and experience the future of ERP-driven product data management. Let’s unify, innovate, and excel together.

For each of the above described three areas where SAP is uniquely positioned to simplify and streamline your end-to-end supply chain processes, we will publish soon more detailed blogs. Stay tuned! For more information about our solutions for product lifecycle management, please visit the SAP website, or join our SAP Community by following the Product Lifecycle Management tag.

entry.title: Table Input Format for Anomaly Detection in HANA_ML
entry.content:
I run into an issue with anomaly detection when using HANA_ML. I have a table in in SAP HANA with numeric columns in format DOUBLE. I created it in two variants:

HC_TABLE1 with just the data

HC_TABLE2 the same data but also having an ID columns

Now training an IsolationForsest with the data works fine and I can save the model:

iso_forest = IsolationForest(
max_samples =2048,
n_estimators=100, # Number of trees
max_features=458, # Use all features from the table
random_state=1, # For reproducibility
thread_ratio=0.9 # Parallel processing
)

hana_df1 = conn.table('HC_TABLE1')

result = iso_forest.fit(hana_df1)

However predicting the outliers does NOT work:

hana_df2 = conn.table('HC_TABLE2')

predictions = iso_forest.predict(hana_df2, key='ID')

ERROR:hana_ml.algorithms.pal.preprocessing:HANA version: 2.00.078.00.1715149848 (fa/hana2sp07). (423, 'AFL error: AFL DESCRIBE for nested call failed - invalid table(s) for ANY-procedure call (Input table 0: column 0 has invalid SQL type.): line 33 col 1 (at pos 8351)')

How can I identify what is going wrong here? I can train a model but never use it? The exactly same data which was used for fitting is not accepted for doing predictions. I am close to ditching HANA_ML completely and using the real python ML routines instead.

entry.title: Re: Remove UOM from value from query result set from FIOR...
entry.content:
You can hide/remove it via local annotations in annotation.xml file.

 

            <Annotations Target="cds_zsd_schd_line.ZC_SCHD_LINEType/CorrectedQtyInOrderQtyUnit">
                <Annotation Term="Measures.Unit" String= " "/>
            </Annotations>

 

by providing Annotation Term "Measures.Unit" String = "" on field itself.

Make sure you provide also the reference namespace in the annotation file:

 

    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://oasis-tcs.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Org.OData.Measures.V1.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="Org.OData.Measures.V1" Alias="Measures"/>
    </edmx:Reference>

 

 Complete annotation.xml file:

 

<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://oasis-tcs.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Org.OData.Measures.V1.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="Org.OData.Measures.V1" Alias="Measures"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZUI_SCHD_LINE/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="cds_zsd_schd_line"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
            <Annotations Target="cds_zsd_schd_line.ZC_SCHD_LINEType/CorrectedQtyInOrderQtyUnit">
                <Annotation Term="Measures.Unit" String= " "/>
            </Annotations>
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

entry.title: Re: Script to extract measure values and loop through the...
entry.content:
Hi there,

As a simple example, the code below will loop through all measure values in the table,
As soon as a value is greater than 282 - the script will launch an application message or a text box etc.
I've created 2 buttons one for each...

var arr = Table_3.getDataSource().getResultSet();

for (var i=0;i<arr.length;i++)
	{   var string = arr[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].rawValue;	    
                    var number = ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(string);		     
if(number>282)   {		Text_1.setVisible(true);	break	   }}	
var arr = Table_3.getDataSource().getResultSet();

for (var i=0;i<arr.length;i++)
	{   var string = arr[i][Alias.MeasureDimension].rawValue;	    
                    var number = ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(string);		     
if(number>282)   {		Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info,"test");	break	   }}

entry.title: Re: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2502
entry.content:
Hi Shrisendu,

Thanks for the question.

User Notification -  This feature enable the user to generate fiori notifications which can be monitored in the APM application's notifications button (Bell Button). In addition to fiori notifications this feature also enables the user to recieve e-mails in the configured e-mail id's.

You can enable user notification by following the below steps.

1. Enable 'User Notification' option in the corresponding rule output. 

2. Configure the e-mail of the reciepients in the corresponding rule settings.

3. To receive e-mail notifications, ensure that a destination is configured. For more information, see  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_APM/8c95dca44fda4d56a17f6494f421758d/baff10b05ab94ad1860abbe7ce5f2825.html 

4. In Fiori Launch pad settings (User) enable the required option (Enabled by default)

Thanks & Regards

Bejoy

entry.title: Read custom fields with SCIM API V2
entry.content:
Hello Community,

we rcently migrated IAS from API V1 to SCIM API V2 and we have custom transformations that do not work anymore.

We used to read 2 custom fields in the Source transformations and then we added conditions in the Target transformations, but it seems that the custom fields are not read anymore.

How can we read custom fields with SCIM API V2?

in the Source transformation we added this:

{
"sourcePath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:successfactors:2.0:User']['IASsync']",
"optional": true,
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:successfactors:2.0:User']['IASsync']"
},

where IASsync is the technical name of the custom field we have in SF.

then in Target trasnformation we added a condition:

($.IASsync != '2046299')

where '2046299' is the picklist OptionID for the value 'NO'. Previoulsy this worked and users with IASsync=NO where skipped and not synced in IAS, which is our objective.

Any help is appreciated since we are stuck with it!

thanks

entry.title: How to build a document flow in onboarding without preventing the completion of the hiring process
entry.content:
During the hiring process, documents like policies or forms should be signed by onboardees. Some of these documents need to be signed to complete the hiring process. The rest can be signed later or in parallel with administrative actions in Manage Pending Hire. Before the 2H2024 release, it was not possible to split the signature steps. Introducing Primary and Secondary Tasks in the program is now possible.

To learn how to configure primary and secondary tasks with document flow, review the blog 

SuccessFactors Onboarding: The New "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" Step 

The key information that you need to know:

  • If a document step is added after secondary tasks, for example, in the program, please add one required custom task in the secondary tasks

  • When the custom task in the secondary tasks is completed, the document step will appear under New Hire Tasks and not in data collection, and it will not prevent the hiring process. Therefore, the onboardee will be available in Manage Pending Hire.

  • If you have two document steps, the first one after data collection to be completed before Manage Pending Hire, and the second one after the secondary tasks, you can assign the documents to the proper document step in the rule using the process variant step name.

entry.title: Re: Base ratio is calculated on the Production Order row...
entry.content:
Hi @Krishna42 ,

For standard components: The Base Ratio is typically the same as the Base Quantity. It is calculated by dividing the component's quantity by the planned quantity of the finished good in the production order header.

For sub-BOM components: The Base Ratio is calculated by dividing the sub-BOM component item quantity over the Finished Goods Item Completed Quantity of the same sub-BOM.

I hope it helps.

*** Please, if this answer resolves your question, kindly consider marking it as accepted. This helps our community by highlighting useful solutions. Thank you! ***

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Why Collaborative Maintenance?
entry.content:
In today's world, many companies are moving from single-tier to multi-tier supply chains. While this shift provides flexibility, cost advantages, and resilience, it also introduces challenges, particularly around connectivity and visibility across suppliers, manufacturers, and service contractors.

  • Data Silos & Inconsistent Standards: Different systems among suppliers complicate data exchange and hinder interoperability. This can lead to significant delays and miscommunications.
  • Lack of Real-Time Visibility: Companies struggle to track assets and maintenance across tiers, resulting in potential disruptions and inefficiencies in the supply chain.
  • Business Partner Onboarding & Integration: Connecting new suppliers is complex, especially for smaller ones with limited technology infrastructure. This complexity can slow down the integration process and affect overall productivity.
  • Collaboration & Communication Gaps: Misalignment between tiers leads to errors and inefficiencies. Effective communication and collaboration are crucial to ensure smooth operations and minimize misunderstandings.

 

How to Overcome These Challenges?

 

SAP Business Network addresses multi-tier maintenance connectivity challenges by offering a collaborative platform for asset operators, OEMs, spare parts suppliers, and service contractors. This innovative platform streamlines maintenance collaboration across various supply chain tiers, enhancing efficiency and reducing potential disruptions.

 

 A multi-tier maintenance network creates value for both asset operators and service contractors by improving visibility, collaboration, and efficiency in maintenance operations. Here’s a breakdown of the value proposition from both perspectives:
 
Asset Operators
  • Automatically share work orders with qualified service contractors to ensure quick response times by connecting with multiple contractors and collaborating on a single source of truth. This approach streamlines communication, reduces delays, and ensures that all parties have access to the same accurate information.
  • Reduce maintenance data management costs by improving the efficiency of data handover processes.
  • Track the exact progress of a maintenance job in real time. Updates include job start time, in-progress status, completion, and documents.
Service Contractors
  • Expand your market reach by connecting with multiple asset operators on a single platform, enhancing your business opportunities and growth potential.
  • Improve wrench time by providing centralized access to all maintenance information, ensuring continuous collaboration among asset ecosystem partners and enhancing overall efficiency.
  • Enhance maintenance practices with accurate and comprehensive technical data to ensure efficiency and accuracy in all maintenance operations.

 Conclusion: Why Use SAP Business Network for Maintenance?

  • Improve asset data accuracy & completeness: Remove the data silos and collaborating on a common model taxonomy and create a consistent understanding.
  • Improve wrench time: Provide centralized access to all maintenance information with continuous collaboration among asset ecosystem partners.
  • Reduce maintenance data management costs: Improve efficiency of data handover from asset owners to service providers and reduce data maintenance and validation.
  • Reduce un-planned downtime or outages: Enable effective collaboration between the operator and service provider on equipment failures and maintenance performance.
  • Improve customer satisfaction: Manage of the full customer lifecycle from maintenance planning, scheduling, execution, and customer service.

 

Thanks for reading the blog post and Stay tuned!

We encourage you to visit the SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration Customer Influence Portal to submit your innovation request...

We hope you enjoy these new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and Stay tuned!

Follow me on LinkedIn

entry.title: The Ultimate Differences Between Order/Workcenter/Operation Activity PODS
entry.content:
I have been researching the final differences between standard PODs within the SAP DM lately. However, I have not been able to find fully satisfying answers.

The general response I have encountered is that the Order POD is used for process production, while Workcenter and Operation Activity PODs are meant for discrete production. However, in the project setups I have worked on, the Order POD can also be used within discrete production scenarios. This raises the question of what the definitive difference between these PODs actually is.

Based on my personal analysis, I have made the following conclusions about the different POD types. If there is a definitive answer to this question, I would greatly appreciate your insights.

Order POD:

  • The Order POD is originally designed for process orders. However, to accommodate discrete orders as well, the "Production Order" option has been introduced in the POD Design section.
  • It meets the needs of most factories and almost covers the features of the Workcenter POD.
  • The primary logic is to manage the entire production process at the order level within a single screen. If all operations are executed within a single work center, once an order is selected, all its operations appear in a list on the left panel. This allows a single operator to start and complete all operations efficiently at the order level.
  • If operations are split across different work centers when the order is created, each operator will only see the relevant operations for their work center.
  • Consumption entries can be directly posted with movement type 261, whereas in other PODs, it is handled through the Assemble function.
  • A key distinguishing feature of the Order POD is that confirmations at the end of operations are not mandatory.

Workcenter POD:

  • Instead of focusing on orders, this POD prioritizes work centers/resources. The assigned SFCs for a particular work center or resource are displayed on the screen.
  • Unlike the Order POD, it includes resource segmentation, allowing operations within the same work center to be managed by different individuals if assigned to different resources.
  • Consumption cannot be posted directly using 261; it must be processed through the Assembly tab. Unlike the Order POD, there is no direct consumption section.
  • Once an operation is completed, confirmation is automatically triggered. This rule is mandatory. (I have also come across information suggesting that if auto-confirmation is disabled in material management or another relevant area, the operation will not be listed here and will instead appear in the Order POD.)

Operation Activity POD:

  • It is very similar to the Workcenter POD but is mainly designed to process multiple SFCs simultaneously.

 

 

In conclusion, as mentioned, these are my personal observations, and I acknowledge that there may be inaccuracies. I would appreciate your insights to clarify the exact distinctions between these PODs.

Thank you in advance for your contributions.

Best regards,
Murat Serhat Yüksel

entry.title: CDS: How to use AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter in Analytical query (Constant Selection)
entry.content:
This blog is about how to use @ AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter in Analytical query to define a constant selection as like in BW query (see Note  944815).

‘Ignore filter’ is a property that influences the effect of filters on cells, in classic BW it is called as ‘Constant selection’. It is used to define a selection in query definition as fixed value. Navigation and filters then have no effect on this selection at runtime.

This feature is useful to calculate e.g., Market Index.  More information about constant selection can be found Constant Selection | SAP Help Portal

 

@ AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter

annotation is used to model 'constant selection' in the Analytical Engine.

The value of each cell in a query result set is determined by different filters acting on this cell. These filters are from the structure element or key figures itself or global filters and drill down.  

Sometimes there is a need that not all filters should be applied, therefore with this annotation filters on certain dimensions or on all dimensions can be excluded.

@AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter.forElement: Global filters and filters from drill down will not be applied on the structure element for the elements listed in the array.

@AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter.forAllElements: No global filters and no filter from drill down will be applied on the structure element.

 

Example

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
@EndUserText.label: 'IGNOREFILTER- constant selection'
@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #ANALYTICAL_QUERY
@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [#ANALYTICAL_QUERY]
define transient view entity ZLR_PC_IGNOREFILTER1
provider contract analytical_query
as projection on ZDMO_I_FLIGHTCUBE
{
  
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS 
  AirlineID,

  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS 
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
  ConnectionID,

  
  CurrencyCode,
   
  
  @EndUserText.label: 'Price in 2024  '
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
   case when FlightYear = '2024' then Price else null end as priceIn2024_without,
   
   
  @EndUserText.label: 'Price in 2024 for Airline'
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter.forElement: ['ConnectionID' ]
  case when FlightYear = '2024' then Price else null end as priceIn2024
 
}
where AirlineID = 'AA' or AirlineID = 'AZ'

 

Output

In the above example column 'Price in 2024 for Airline' shows the total price for airline, as 'ConnectionID' filter is ignored with annotation @AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter.forElement: ['ConnectionID' ]

Constraint

This feature is only available in Analytical query

entry.title: Re: Archiving of materials in S4 Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi AbhijitGoswami 

I would recommend to fully review the ILM/Archive options.

You would likely not be able to bring them back into the system from the Archive. You should be able to view the archived records with the Archive Explorer app

There are few apps that can look into the archive, but they have to have that functionality, for example

Display Billing Document (VF03)
Manage Supplier Line Items (FBL1N)
Display Document Flow (F3665)
DisplayPurchase Order Advanced

I think very few (PO) can bring them back from the archive.

So if you intend to use them later, I would recommend not to archive them.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: New Review Order Progress - Multilevel View App
entry.content:
Exciting news coming to you in the area of manufacturing with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1 Release. The new app Review Order Progress - Multilevel View (F7681) is finally here. 

 

Review Order Progress - Multilevel View App (F7681)

 

What is the Purpose of this App? 

With this app, you can review the progress of a selected sales order, production order, or process order. The app displays a multilevel view of all relevant component requirements and their receipt elements, as well as additional information, for example, about shortages or other issues that might occur for each component.

This app is contained in the business catalog Production Planning - MRP Cockpit (SAP_SCM_BC_MRPCOCKPIT_MC).  

 

Summary of key Features

This app provides the following key features:

  • Display the component requirements and receipt elements, as well as all associated requirements and receipts for the selected order on multiple levels of the order structure. Supported order types include sales order, process order, or production orders.

  • Check the progress of production or procurement for a particular order. Check whether there are shortages or other issues with meeting requirements for any component at any level.

  • Show the delay of a specific component, referred to as the component delay. It also shows the total delay of an order. The total delay is calculated based on a proposed requirement date for each component. This date takes into account the requirement date of the selected order as well as different scheduling parameters, for example, the planned delivery time or in-house production time of superordinate components.

  • Navigate to other apps for further processing or monitoring from the material, requirement element or receipt element, for example, Manage Material Coverage, Display Material Stock, Display Sales Order, Display Planned Order, Change Planned Order, or Display Purchase Requisition.

  • Export the component structure with all relevant information into a Microsoft Excel file.

How do I use this app? 

Your Entry Point - Header Level

You start at header level to specify all filter parameters. Indicated with a red star are all mandatory filter fields:  

Entry Level Header Level - Filter Possibilities

Document Selection  

You can specify, enter or select the order type & number of the sales order, production order, or process order that you want to display:

Header Level - Document Type & Number

 

  • For Sales orders, simply type the order number directly into the input field or use the value help.

  • For Process or Production Orders, open the value help dialog and select the Process and Production Order search template.

    • You can then search for your document and select it from the results list

Search

First you can enter an exact search term or use the generic search by just entering part of the term.

Header Level - Search

The results list includes all possible matches for the following columns:

  • Material
  • Plant
  • MRP Controller

Issues Only

You can use the filter Issues Only to display the order structure only for those components for which issues have been identified:

Header Level - Issues Only Yes / No

 

 

 

  • Select Yes to restrict the displayed order structure to those components that have identified production or procurement issues, including their superordinate components.

  • Select No to display the complete order structure including all components, regardless of whether they have issues.

Order Component Level 

Once you have filtered the order structure the action happens on the order component level. Let's look at the information you can gain. 

Material 

The column displays the material number and description of the components that are included in the Bill - of - Material (BOM) in the selected order. If a component is included multiple times within the Bill - of - Material (BOM), for example, if it is required for several sub-components, this column includes multiple entries for the same material.

Order Component Level - Material

 

The material components are displayed in a multilevel view to indicate the structure of the Bill - of - Material (BOM) in the selected order. Requirement and receipt information including coverage issues are displayed for each component.

When you use a special planning procedure for materials, as defined in the material master data, the materials may not display in the order tree. For instance, consider the MRP Type ND. This MRP type stands for "No Planning". In such cases, the materials aren't displayed in the order tree.

From the Material Number column, you can also open a quick view for each component to access additional information such as material details, MRP data, stock and shortage information.

Order Component View - Related Actions for Material

 

Issues 

This column displays the number of issues and warnings that were detected for each requirement included in the selected order, as well as their criticality. It also indicates whether issues exist on the subordinate structure levels of each component.

 

Order Component Level - Issues

The system detects the following issues and indicates their criticality, using different colors and icons:

Warnings (non critical issues) are shown in yellow:

  • Opening Date in the Past - The opening date of a receipt element occurs in the past.
  • Start Date in the Past - The basic start date of a receipt element occurs in the past.

 

Order Component Level - Issue Warning

Errors (critical issues) are shown in red:

 

Order Component Level - Errors

 The errors can be the following:

  • Finish Date in the Past (The basic end date or the delivery date of a receipt element occurs in the past):

Error - Finish Date in the Past 

  • Reschedule In
    • A receipt should be rescheduled to an earlier date to avoid a material shortage.
    • Check if it is possible to bring forward (reschedule in) the availability date for the receipt in the Manage Material Coverage app.
  • Coverage not Provided by Master Plan
    • There is insufficient coverage because a planned receipt does not cover the required quantity at all or it covers the required quantity only partially.

You can click the icon to access a list of all detected issues for a selected component and to sort them by criticality (error / warning). To access a list of all issues that exist for an order, click the icon that is displayed for the top-level node of the structure. The number displayed within this icon indicates the sum of all errors and warnings that were detected for the order.

Requirement Element 

This column displays the requirement elements that exist for each material component.

Order Component Level - Requirement Element

If more than one requirement exists for a material within an order, a separate row is displayed for each requirement element.

The comparison of requirement and receipt elements helps to analyze the demand and supply situation for each material component. The following types of requirement elements can be included in the table:

  • CusOrd (Sales Order)
  • DepReq (Dependent Requirement)
  • OrdDS (Release of Stock Transfer Order)
  • OrdRes (Dependent Reservation)
  • PRqRel (Release of Stock Transfer Requisition)

You can click on a requirement element to open a quick view with more detailed information or to navigate to other apps for information about related objects or further processing. For example, you can access the Display Sales Order, Display Planned Order or Change Planned Order apps using the Related Actions link in the quickview.

Order Component Level - Requirement Element Related Actions

 

Proposed Requirement Date

Shows the date at which the required quantity of each component needs to be on hand to make sure the selected order can be fulfilled on time.

Order Component Level - Proposed Requirement Date

To determine this date, the system considers the requirement date of the selected sales order, process order, or production order as well as different scheduling parameters that are defined in the material master data. 

The proposed requirement date helps you determine whether a specific component is actually required earlier or later than the date specified in its requirement document. To calculate it, the system uses the requirement date of the selected order and additionally considers different scheduling parameters, depending on the procurement type of each component material:

  • For components with a superordinate component that is procured externally, the system subtracts the goods receipt processing time in working days and the planned delivery time in calendar days from the proposed requirement date of the superordinate component. The resulting date is the proposed requirement date for the selected component.
  • For components with a superordinate component that is produced in-house, the system subtracts the goods receipt processing time and the in-house production time in working days from the proposed requirement date of the superordinate component. The resulting date is the proposed requirement date for the selected component.

Requirement Date

The requirement date comes from the documents itself, e.g. from the requirement date of a sales order: 

Requirement Date

 

Receipt Element

This column displays the receipt elements that exist for each requirement of a material component.

Order Component Level - Receipt Element

The comparison of requirement and receipt elements helps to analyze the demand and supply situation for each material component. The following types of receipt elements can be included in the table:

  • PldOrd (Planned Order)
  • POitem (Purchase Order Item/ Stock Transport Order Item)
  • PrcOrd (Process Order)
  • PrdOrd (Production Order)
  • PurRqs (Purchase Requisition)
  • SchLne (Schedule Lines)
  • Stock (Stock)

If more than one receipt element is related to a requirement, the system displays the most critical MRP element (the most delayed element) and a link that you can click on to display an overview of all receipt elements assigned to the requirement.

Order Component Level - Receipt Element Link

 

Note that this overview is automatically sorted by Availability Date first, then by Covered Quantity and Receipt Element. You cannot change this sorting.

Clicking on a specific MRP element opens a quickview with additional details on the element, as well as options to navigate to other apps for information about related objects or for further processing. For example, you can access the Display Sales Order, Display Planned Order , or Change Planned Order apps using the Related Actions link in the quickview.

Order Component Level - Receipt Element Related Actions

 

Availability Date

This column displays the date at which the quantity of a receipt element is available.

Order Component Level - Availability Date

If more than one receipt element is available for a requirement, the availability date of the most critical receipt element (the one with the greatest delay) is displayed in this column.

If the availability date falls after a component's requirement date, the system automatically calculates and shows the delay in the Component Delay column.

Order Component Level - Availability Date to Component Delay

Uncovered Quantity

The uncovered quantity is the part of the order quantity that can neither be covered on time (covered quantity) nor with a delay (late quantity).

Order Component Level - Uncovered Quantity

 

The uncovered quantity is determined by subtracting the late quantity from the missing quantity.

Missing Quantity

Specifies the part of the component quantity that is missing in order to cover a specific requirement.

Order Component Level - Missing Quantity

The missing quantity includes both late and uncovered quantities and is determined by subtracting the covered quantity from the required quantity.

Component Delay 

Displays the number of working days that the availability of a receipt quantity is delayed for an individual component.

Order Component Level - Component Delay

For each receipt element of a component, the system compares the Requirement Date with the Availability Date to calculate the number of days it is delayed. If the Availability Date falls after the Requirement Date, the difference is shown as delay for the component and it is highlighted in red.

If there is more than one receipt element with a delay for the same requirement, the most critical delay (the highest value) is displayed in this column. To display the component delay for each receipt element individually, you can click the more link in the Receipt Element column.

Total Delay

Displays the maximum delay that is determined for the receipt elements of all subordinate components at a specific level of the order structure.

Order Component Level - Total Delay

The value that is displayed in this column helps you determine how much the components of an order structure are actually delayed, based on the individual delays of their underlying components. Since the requirement of a selected order cannot be fulfilled before all subordinate material components are available, the total delay on the top level of the order structure always matches the maximum delay that was determined for the lower levels of the structure. The same applies to component requirements at lower levels of the order structure, if these have subordinate component requirements assigned.

To determine the total delay at a specific component level, the system compares the Proposed Requirement Date with the Availability Date and calculates the number of working days that each receipt element is delayed. If more than one receipt element exists for a requirement, it determines the maximum delay of all receipts. As a second step, the system checks if the total delay of any receipt element at a lower level of the order structure is greater and displays the maximum delay value in this column.

Late Quantity

The late quantity is the part of the order quantity that can be covered at a later point in time, for example, because it first needs to be provided by a supplier and is received only after the requirement date.

Order Component Level - Late Quantity

 The late quantity is determined by subtracting the uncovered quantity from the missing quantity.

Covered Quantity

Specifies the part of the order quantity that has not yet been issued to the customer, but can be covered in time, for example, based on the available stock or planned receipts.

Order Component Level - Covered Quantity

entry.title: Belgium- Annual Sales List for Company Codes with Local Currency "USD"
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a query regarding to Belgium Annual Sales List.

Definition of this report:

As you can see, reporting currency is set to "EUR" for this report. 

But we have company codes with Local currency as "USD" and because of which this report does not work.

Our Current SAP version is S/4 2022. Earlier we had S/4H 1909 and used Transaction code with "Alternate Reporting Currency" option. This report is obsolete in current S/4H version.

Is there way to handle this scenario?

In one of our test environment, we created a new report copying the existing BE_SL_ANNUAL & changed the Reporting currency as "USD". We are able run the report for Company codes with Local currency "USD"  & download the XML but obliviously values are in USD.

Please let us know if there is option to setup report for Company codes with Local Currency "USD" & get output in currency "EUR".  

Regards,

Avinash

entry.title: Connectivity within cross-regions and cross-subaccounts
entry.content:
Use case

You have subscribed to SAP Build Apps in EU(Frankfurt) region and SAP Build Process Automation in Singapore region. As per the use case, an application built in SAP Build Apps calls a process in SAP Build Process Automation. Based on the use case, "Subaccount 1" should be the Build Apps subaccount (EU(Frankfurt)) and "Subaccount 2" should be your SAP Build Process Automation subaccount (Singapore).

Prerequisites

  1. Service Key is available for SAP Build Process Automation instance.
  2. Make sure that your process in SAP Build Process Automation, is deployed. You have the trigger with the similar Payload as shown in the image below. You will need this information in the last step while configuring REST API in SAP Build Apps. Note the following information in the notepad, we will need this information in next steps:
  • URL: We will refer to the URL mentioned in the API Trigger popup box as ${API_TRIGGER_SBPA_URL}. This will be used in the destination.
  • definitionId: This will be referred to as ${API_TRIGGER_SBPA_DEFINITIONID}. It will be used in the REST API call.
  • Rest of the payload information: The same structure will be created in the REST API in SAP Build Apps. For example, in this case,

"context":{ "_name":"",

"age":""

}

 

Procedure

This scenario can be achieved in 3 steps as explained below:

  • Setup trust between the 2 subaccounts,
  • Configure destination in Subaccount 1,
  • Configure REST API in SAP Build Apps

Setup trust between the 2 subaccounts

Now, to build the trust follow the steps given in the link below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/set-up-trust-between-systems

Configure Destination

After the trust has been established, you will need to create the destination. Follow this excellent and highly detailed guide:

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/user-propagation-between-cloud-foundry-applications?locale=en-US#loio8ebf60c82a8e4cfc904f441c0c0acd6b__assemble

In destination, fill-in the information as given the help document above and also refer to the points mentioned below for additional tips.

  • URL: ${API_TRIGGER_SBPA_URL}
  • Authentication: OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion
  • Key Store Location: <blank>
  • Key Store password: <blank>
  • Client Key: clientid to be retrieved from service key created for SAP Build Process Automation instance in Subaccount 2
  • Token Service User: clientid to be retrieved from service key created for SAP Build Process Automation instance in Subaccount 2
  • Token Service Password: clientsecret to be retrieved from service key created for SAP Build Process Automation instance in Subaccount 2

Additional Properties:

  • AppgyverEnabled: true
  • BuildApps.ApiType: true
  • BuildApps.Enabled: true
  • BuildApps.ApiType: rest
  • CEP.EnableCsrfProtection: true
  • HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
  • nameIdFormat: urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress
  • sap.cloud.service: com.sap.spa.processautomation
  • sap.cloud.service.alias: spa
  • WebIDEEnabled: true

Apart from nameIDFormat, make sure to add rest all the other additional parameters in the destination. The connection check for the destination should result in 401: Unauthorized. The destination is now ready.

Configure REST API within SAP Build Apps

Open SAP Build Apps and navigate to Integration > SAP Systems > Add Integration > Choose your destination > Install Integration > Add New REST API Integration. Next steps to follow are described in the below link under section REST API Integration in Build Apps.

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-build-process-automation#rest-api-integration-in-sap-build-apps

Here, make sure to create the same structure as you have in the payload in SAP Build Process Automation API trigger. definitionId will be ${API_TRIGGER_SBPA_DEFINITIONID} as mentioned in the API trigger.

After the process is trigger successfully, you can see the process within your SAP Build Process Automation tenant under Monitoring > Manage > Processes and Workflows tile.

TIP:

  • In case of any error, check network logs. They give the best information.

entry.title: Different Papersize between CR Designer and RAS
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm using CR Designer 2020 SP4 Patch 1000 and Crystal Reports for Visual Studio SP 37, so the latest versions of both.

I have a label printer "Zebra ZDesigner ZD220-203dpi ZPL".
When using the Page Setup in CRD, "User Defined Size" is the only option:

So when I save the report with these settings and open it with my .Net program (VS 2022), 
for
System.Drawing.Printing.PrintDocument.PrinterSettings.PaperSizes
I can see these values:

  • "User defined" instead of "User Defined Size" as papername
  • 256 (ReportDefModel.CrPaperSizeEnum.crPaperSizeUser)
    instead of
    0 (ReportDefModel.CrPaperSizeEnum.crPaperSizeDefault) for Rawkind

Does the designer have a special logic when using Dissociate with papersizes?
Why is there a difference?

I have the same behavior with Don's test program.

BTW. similar problem with page orientation: the designer saves a portrait, RAS reads landscape - a known bug?

Regards,

Patrick

entry.title: Re: Subscription Billing - Split Bill
entry.content:
Hi Navneet,

there are many fields that lead to a split in the standard billing logic of Subscription Billing:
https://help.sap.com/docs/subscription-billing/feature-overview/aggregation-and-splitting

In particular, the customer (=sold-to), payer and bill-to party lead to a bill split automatically.
If there's further criteria, the bill split element is the way for custom logic, that should be considered on top.

Please also be aware, that the default logic for integration to Convergent Invoicing in S/4HANA Cloud, public edition splits by subscription as well... But you still can converge multiple bills of multiple subscriptions in one invoice in the S/4 invoicing process.

Best regards
Stefan
